notes.discrepancy_explanation: Finding 067 counted 57,676 unique Form IDs by deduplicating across voice type variants (e.g., Beckett has devgreen/devpurple/dlc1/main copies with identical Form IDs). This analysis counts 101,208 Form IDs across all 1,044 actor folders including duplicates. The orphan identification methodology is the same: any voice Form ID not present as a QUST:INFO record in the ESM is orphaned. Duplicate voice type folders inflate both the total and orphaned counts proportionally.
duplicate_voice_type_examples: w05_npcm_comp_beckett_main, _devgreen, _devpurple, _dlc1 (4 copies, same 1287 Form IDs each)
w05_npcf_astronaut_sofiadaguerre has similar duplicate voice type folders
ba2_source_archives: SeventySix - Voices.ba2 (launch archive, bulk of orphans)

string_matches.text: Now according to that map, the treasure is in some kind of cache inside Gauley Mine, sealed away behind a door. A door that card unlocks.
Well, well.
So you were really doing me a favor, executing that boy. What a hero.
Player killed squirrel.
Get back here! You're my dinner!
... you sure you still want to let him go?
After college, I went to the D.C. area and applied for the astronaut program a couple times until I was accepted. My folks were so proud...
Just wish I could contribute more, to be honest.
Now, Crane had a map and an access card he kept on him.
Atta boy.
You two. You're right. But this is going to take some time.
You want someone to get hurt? Show me.
Hmm... other than that... I had a few relationships. Nothing serious.
Starting Scene
Unregistered access detected. Deploying counter measures.
Summersville, huh? Good bots will fetch a pretty penny. Fine. You got yourself a deal.
Ah, wow... Thank you so much for killing that thing! You don't know how long it's been chasing me...
Though I do wish maybe you hadn't struck a deal with them in the first place.
You're gonna taste so good!
Heh heh... Alright, let's see what we got here.
That things been up here this whole time?!
Civic duties are their own reward.
But look, I didn't choose to sit out here and watch the bombs go off in my bathrobe like some venerable old peach. No, I was left here to die because I'm old and mean and that's fine by me.
Oww, oww.
Reinitiating defense of the proletariat.
I'll wait for you in the big cabin where we stayed during the field trip and maybe we can talk about it, okay?
This is my first interview with another survivor, Kesha McDermott.
Big Bend Tunnel Mining Operation. Update for Day 163
Are you wearing a wire? A goddamn wire?
She found me trying to break into a Nuka-Cola machine and showed me a different way.
Tell whoever will listen that I am offering twice what they were making hauling coal to come up to New Gad and lend a hand.
Removes 75 rads.
Small chance to send targets flying.
Target reacquired! Communists - to action!
To hell with your sympathy. I'm going out on an IV full of painkillers and you're just jealous that you didn't think of it first. Sucker.
Running elimination subroutines!
What? What's that?
Hunter/Hunted: You have been eliminated! (Kill count: <Variable=KillCount>)
Precinct unresponsive. Request for repairs failed.
I like things how I like them, and I am not going to change one bit for anyone. I recorded this just in case anyone saw my body and felt bad for me.
Well, I'll be damned. Good work, boys and girls. Seems you've got some real mettle after all.
Enemy of the party in sight. Reengaging.
Change sections with [Right] or [Left].
You can purchase new goods and sell your spoils by bartering with merchants throughout the Commonwealth.
He seems to be stuck. He is not functioning properly.
Hardware malfunction detected. Unit is immobile.

Stay alive longer by wearing Armor. Armor provides Damage Resistance, which reduces the damage you'll suffer from incoming attacks. You can purchase armor from merchants or loot it from your fallen foes.
My men are trying their best, but I continue to urge the need for proper ventilation.
I'm hungry. For human.
But look, I didn't choose to sit out here and watch the bombs go off in my bathrobe like some venerable old peach. No, I was left here to die because I'm old and mean and that's fine by me.
Oww, oww.
I'll wait for you in the big cabin where we stayed during the field trip and maybe we can talk about it, okay?
This is my first interview with another survivor, Kesha McDermott.

BOOM!

After college, I went to the D.C. area and applied for the astronaut program a couple times until I was accepted. My folks were so proud...
Just wish I could contribute more, to be honest.
Now, Crane had a map and an access card he kept on him.
Atta boy.
You two. You're right. But this is going to take some time.
You want someone to get hurt? Show me.
Hmm... other than that... I had a few relationships. Nothing serious.
Starting Scene
Unregistered access detected. Deploying counter measures.
Summersville, huh? Good bots will fetch a pretty penny. Fine. You got yourself a deal.
Ah, wow... Thank you so much for killing that thing! You don't know how long it's been chasing me...
Though I do wish maybe you hadn't struck a deal with them in the first place.
You're gonna taste so good!
Heh heh... Alright, let's see what we got here.
That things been up here this whole time?!

analysis_summary.total_orphan_rate: 20.1% of voice Form IDs have no INFO record
analysis_summary.fo4_legacy_dominance: 82.4% of orphans (per finding 067) are generic FO4 legacy voice types never connected to FO76
analysis_summary.named_npc_orphans: 17.6% of orphans belong to named NPCs with cut quest/dialogue content
key_findings: Beckett has 156 orphaned Form IDs (12.1%) - cut Poseidon Power Plant finale with dual villain (The Bone + The Claw)
ZAX (Nuclear Winter AI) has 90 orphaned Form IDs (15.9%) - 32% of commentary never deployed
Sofia Daguerre has 87 orphaned Form IDs (6.0%) - cut companion personal quest content
MODUS has 73 orphaned Form IDs (13.7%) - cut Enclave quest branches
Duchess has 37 orphaned Form IDs (6.8%) - cut Wayward tavern dialogue
Ronny has 32 orphaned Form IDs (50%) - half her dialogue was cut with Beckett's restructured finale
Ryan Ainsley: 11 lines, 100% orphaned - ghost architect of Whitespring Springhill golf course
Moe the Mole: 8 lines, 100% orphaned - complete 16-line script survives in strings
Paladin Radio: 5 Form IDs (45 files), 100% orphaned - cut BoS radio station with full infrastructure
Survey Bot / Drill Sergeant / CBZ13 Robotic Eyebot: military robots 100% orphaned
216 orphaned Form IDs have matching text in ilstrings/dlstrings - these are coincidental Form ID collisions with unrelated string entries, not actual dialogue text for these voice lines
analysis_summary.string_match_analysis: The 216 string matches are almost entirely false positives - the same Form ID numbers that appear as orphaned voice file names (e.g., 000439ce) also happen to be used as string IDs in the ilstrings/dlstrings tables for completely unrelated content (holotape transcripts, UI text, item descriptions). This is because the string table uses its own ID space. The voice files and strings use different ID namespaces that occasionally overlap. The ONE confirmed real match is Moe the Mole's quest (SF07_MoeDigsSafety), where the dialogue text survives because the CUT quest's INFO records still contain NAM1 string pointers - not because the voice Form IDs matched string IDs.
analysis_summary.expansion_with_most_cuts: Wastelanders (W05 prefix) - 1,092 orphaned Form IDs across 44 actors
analysis_summary.cleanest_archives: Update archives 00-09, 12 have 0% orphan rates - post-launch pipeline is clean
